problem_52.natural_language: If `K` is a nonempty compact set and `U` is open with `K ⊆ U`, then some closed `delta`-neighborhood of `K` is contained in `U`.
problem_52.lean:
theorem exists_delta_pos_closed_neighborhood_subset
    {X : Type*} [MetricSpace X] (K U : Set X)
    (hK : IsCompact K) (hU : IsOpen U) (hKU : K ⊆ U) (hKne : K.Nonempty) :
    ∃ delta > 0, {x : X | Metric.infDist x K ≤ delta} ⊆ U
problem_52.natural_language_proof:
Proof: the complement `Uᶜ` is closed. Since `K` is nonempty compact and `K ∩ Uᶜ = ∅`, the distance
between `K` and `Uᶜ` is positive: there exists `delta > 0` such that `delta ≤ dist x y` for all
`x ∈ K` and `y ∈ Uᶜ`. Set `d = delta` and take `δ = d/2`. Suppose `Metric.infDist x K ≤ δ`. By the
definition of `infDist`, we can choose `k ∈ K` with `dist x k < Metric.infDist x K + δ/2 ≤ δ + δ/2 = 3d/4`.
If `x ∉ U`, then `x ∈ Uᶜ`, so the separation gives `d ≤ dist k x`, contradicting `dist k x < d`.
Hence `x ∈ U`, i.e. `{x | infDist x K ≤ δ} ⊆ U`.
